title: Senior ServiceNow AI Engineer
description:
We’re building a world of health around every individual —
shaping a more connected, convenient and compassionate
health experience. At CVS Health®, you’ll be surrounded by
passionate colleagues who care deeply, innovate with
purpose, hold ourselves accountable and prioritize safety
and quality in everything we do. Join us and be part of
something bigger – helping to simplify health care one
person, one family and one community at a time.

POSITION SUMMARY

CVS Health is seeking a ServiceNow AI Engineer, a platform
engineering role responsible for enabling, scaling, and
operating enterprise AI capabilities across the ServiceNow
platform. This role focuses on Now Assist and Moveworks
integration, delivering secure, governed, and reusable AI
services that can be consumed consistently across HRSD,
ITSM, ITOM, and broader ESM domains.

Operating within the ServiceNow Platform Engineering
organization, this role emphasizes configuration‑first AI
enablement, standardized patterns, and guardrails—ensuring
AI solutions are scalable, measurable, and aligned with
enterprise security, privacy, and governance standards.

Role Scope & Operating Model

- Scope: Platform‑wide (shared AI services across all
  ServiceNow products)
- Model: Central Platform Engineering (not embedded in a
  single product team)
- Primary AI Capabilities: Now Assist Skills, Now Assist
  Agents, Now Assist Agentic Workflows, Moveworks Agent to
  Agent Integration, AI Control Tower, Now Assist for
  Creator
- Engineering Approach: Configuration‑first, with selective
  scripting and integration development as needed
- Success Metrics (Examples)

- Adoption and enablement of Now Assist and Moveworks across
  ServiceNow domains
- Reduction in manual handling time through AI assistance
- Self‑service containment and deflection improvements
- Stability and upgrade safety of AI configurations
- Compliance with AI governance and audit requirements

What we expect of you

1) Enterprise AI Enablement (Now Assist & Moveworks)

- Configure, deploy, and operate Now Assist capabilities
  (Skills, Agents, Agentic Workflows, Agent to Agent
  Integration, AI Control Tower, Now Assist for Creator).
- Integrate and align Moveworks with ServiceNow experiences,
  ensuring seamless handoff, consistent intents, and
  enterprise‑approved patterns.
- Develop within platform‑level AI standards for enterprise
  re-use and scalability.
- Support Citizen Developer model for AI Enablement.
- Support ESM SDLC process (Development, Testing, Code
  Migration, Deployment)

2) Platform Architecture & Reusable Patterns

- Design and maintain reusable AI configurations, including
  Skills and Agents.
- Define reference architectures for common use cases across
  AI configurations.
- Ensure AI solutions align with ServiceNow platform
  architecture, performance expectations, and upgrade
  compatibility.

3) Configuration‑First Engineering with Selective Scripting

- Leverage out‑of‑the‑box and configurable AI features
  wherever possible to maximize maintainability and upgrade
  safety.
- Use scripting and custom logic only when required to extend
  capabilities, integrate systems, or enforce enterprise
  guardrails.
- Build and maintain integrations using Integration Hub, APIs,
  and event‑driven patterns to support AI‑initiated actions.

4) Responsible AI, Security, and Governance

- Responsible AI Governance to ensure:
  - Appropriate data access boundaries
  - Role‑based controls and logging
  - Human‑in‑the‑loop and escalation patterns
- Support architecture reviews, AI technical governance
  boards, and risk assessments for AI features.
- Document AI designs, data sources, and controls to support
  audit and compliance requirements.

5) Measurement, Optimization, and Observability

- Support the definition of and monitoring of platform‑level
  AI KPIs.
- Continuously tune prompts, intents, and configurations based
  on usage data and outcomes.
- Partner with product teams to validate AI value realization
  across domains.

6) Platform Operations & Lifecycle Management

- Establish operational runbooks for AI capabilities,
  including monitoring, incident response, and problem
  management.
- Manage AI feature lifecycle across environments (lower →
  production), including testing, releases, and upgrades.
- Evaluate new ServiceNow AI features and vendor enhancements,
  providing recommendations for platform adoption.

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

- 5+ years of ServiceNow platform engineering or development
  experience.
- 3+ years of experience in ServiceNow configuration, security
  model, data structures, scripting and integration
  patterns.
- 3+ years of experience working in a central platform or
  shared services model.
- At least 1+ years of experience enabling Now Assist or
  AI‑assisted ServiceNow capabilities in production.
- At least 1+ years of experience in translating enterprise AI
  strategy into practical, governed platform capabilities.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience integrating or operating Moveworks with
  ServiceNow.
- Experience with knowledge management, service catalog
  design, and intent modeling.
- Familiarity with AI governance, privacy, and enterprise risk
  management concepts.
- Experience supporting multiple ServiceNow domains (HRSD,
  ITSM, ITOM, ESM).
- Technical Skills

- ServiceNow: Now Assist (all capabilities), Flow Designer,
  IntegrationHub, Performance Analytics, Advanced Scripting
- AI Enablement: prompt configuration, intent/topic modeling,
  fallback and escalation design, evaluation metrics
- Integration: REST APIs, OAuth, data mapping, event‑based
  workflows
- Engineering Practices: configuration management, CI/CD
  awareness, documentation, upgrade readiness.

- Certifications

- ServiceNow Certified System Administrator (CSA)
- ServiceNow Application Developer
- ServiceNow Implementation Specialist (any major domain)
- ServiceNow AI‑related credentials (where available)
- Enterprise AI governance or cloud AI fundamentals training

- Key Partnerships

- Platform Architecture & Engineering
- Product Owners across HRSD, ITSM, ITOM, ESM
- Security, Risk, and Compliance
- AI Technical Governance / Architecture Review Boards
- Service Operations & Knowledge Management

EDUCATION

Bachelor’s degree from accredited university or equivalent
work experience (HS diploma + 4 years relevant experience).

BUSINESS OVERVIEW

Bring your heart to CVS Health Every one of us at CVS Health
shares a single, clear purpose: Bringing our heart to every
moment of your health. This purpose guides our commitment to
deliver enhanced human-centric health care for a rapidly
changing world. Anchored in our brand — with heart at its
center — our purpose sends a personal message that how we
deliver our services is just as important as what we
deliver.  Our Heart At Work Behaviors™ support this purpose.
We want everyone who works at CVS Health to feel empowered
by the role they play in transforming our culture and
accelerating our ability to innovate and deliver solutions
to make health care more personal, convenient and
affordable.  We strive to promote and sustain a culture of
diversity, inclusion and belonging every day.  CVS Health is
an affirmative action employer, and is an equal opportunity
employer, as are the physician-owned businesses for which
CVS Health provides management services. We do not
discriminate in recruiting, hiring, promotion, or any other
personnel action based on race, ethnicity, color, national
origin, sex/gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or
expression, religion, age, disability, protected veteran
status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable
federal, state, or local law.  We proudly support and
encourage people with military experience (active, veterans,
reservists and National Guard) as well as military spouses
to apply for CVS Health job opportunities.

Anticipated Weekly Hours

40

Time Type

Full time

Pay Range

The typical pay range for this role is:

$101,970.00 - $203,940.00

This pay range represents the base hourly rate or base
annual full-time salary for all positions in the job grade
within which this position falls.  The actual base salary
offer will depend on a variety of factors including
experience, education, geography and other relevant factors.
This position is eligible for a CVS Health bonus, commission
or short-term incentive program in addition to the base pay
range listed above.


Our people fuel our future. Our teams reflect the customers,
patients, members and communities we serve and we are
committed to fostering a workplace where every colleague
feels valued and that they belong.

Great benefits for great people

We take pride in offering a comprehensive and competitive
mix of pay and benefits that reflects our commitment to our
colleagues and their families.

This full‑time position is eligible for a comprehensive
benefits package designed to support the physical,
emotional, and financial well‑being of colleagues and their
families. The benefits for this position include medical,
dental, and vision coverage, paid time off, retirement
savings options, wellness programs, and other resources,
based on eligibility.

Additional details about available benefits are provided
during the application process and on Benefits Moments.

We anticipate the application window for this opening will
close on: 06/30/2026

Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will
be considered for employment in accordance with all federal,
state and local laws.
responsibilities: Configure, deploy, and operate Now Assist AI capabilities including Skills, Agents, Agentic Workflows, and AI Control Tower across the ServiceNow platform
Integrate and align Moveworks with ServiceNow experiences ensuring seamless handoff and enterprise-approved patterns
Design and maintain reusable AI configurations and reference architectures for common use cases across HRSD, ITSM, ITOM, and ESM domains
Implement responsible AI governance including data access boundaries, role-based controls, logging, and human-in-the-loop escalation patterns
Tune prompts, intents, and configurations based on usage data and monitor platform-level AI KPIs
Establish operational runbooks for AI capabilities including monitoring, incident response, and problem management
Manage AI feature lifecycle across environments including testing, releases, and upgrades
Support Citizen Developer model for AI enablement and ESM SDLC process
use_cases: Enterprise AI-assisted IT service management (ITSM) with automated incident resolution
HR service delivery (HRSD) automation through conversational AI and self-service
IT operations management (ITOM) with AI-driven event and alert handling
Enterprise service management (ESM) self-service containment and deflection
Agent-to-agent AI integration between Moveworks and ServiceNow for seamless multi-system workflows
AI-assisted creator and developer productivity on the ServiceNow platform
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves directly configuring, deploying, and
operating AI capabilities (Now Assist Skills, Agents,
Agentic Workflows) on the ServiceNow platform, including
prompt configuration, intent modeling, and AI agent-to-agent
integration. While it's a configuration-first approach
rather than model training, the engineer is hands-on
building and deploying AI/agent systems, tuning prompts and
intents, and designing agentic workflows — making it ai-
first.
